in github actions you can run on arm64 or amd64 runners in separate jobs within the same github action - then a job that depends_on both of them to complete and compares their outputs

referenced_message.content: each run appends platform totals into a csv or json blob, script exits with status code when a regression happens. Maybe prune it to last 10 runs per target platform.

content: Should be in the .GitHub folder

bar game has some integration tests, where the first job writes the results to disk, and the second, separate job reads them and pretty-prints them and marks itself as success or failure 

So you could feed that into the LLM for context
each row has the PR / commit hash, contriburtor (for blaming), test a-e results, and date time
each run appends platform totals into a csv or json blob, script exits with status code when a regression happens. Maybe prune it to last 10 runs per target platform.
maybe there're even easier ways, got to ask LLMs
ah yeah indeed
yeah, it can dump the results in a structured format in a bucket somewhere or in the repo, and then the script can do a compare and fail on regressions
Yes, I think so, it runs in a second, but we can't run it cross arch, so we can only post hashes or something, hoping someone cares. Actually there could be a way to compare cross platform runs

content: Geometry will be transfered to the model level. Pieces will retain volume (for collision), but mostly remain bones
Pieces used to serve dual purpose: store geometry and act as bones, this will soon change

Not much changed as far as animations. I optimized it a little bit (unsure if 100% correctly), but otherwise if you rotate on your shoulder joint the rest of your arm matrices need to be updated as well (up to the finger level)
